expectations.text: Document header contains the exact Spec ID CUST-O2C-03
expectations.evidence: Header table first row: 'Spec ID: CUST-O2C-03'.

expectations.text: Script type is identified as Client Script
expectations.evidence: Type field: 'Client Script (CS)'. Design rationale explains why CS chosen over UE and Workflow.

expectations.text: Trigger is fieldChanged or sublistChanged on the Sales Order item sublist
expectations.evidence: §2 Trigger: 'sublistChanged — fires after any change to the item sublist.' pageInit also included for edit mode. Both target the item sublist.

expectations.text: The source field custitem_lead_time_days is explicitly referenced in the affected fields table or pseudocode
expectations.evidence: §3 table: 'Lead Time Days, custitem_lead_time_days, Integer'. Referenced throughout §4.1 pseudocode via search.lookupFields.

expectations.text: Business logic section contains pseudocode that iterates lines and selects the maximum lead time value
expectations.evidence: §4.1: 'FOR EACH entry IN itemIds: days = leadTimeMap[entry.itemId]; IF days IS NOT NULL AND days > 0: IF days > maxLeadTime: maxLeadTime = days'

expectations.text: Edge cases section addresses null lead time, single line scenario, and line removal
expectations.evidence: 15 edge cases. EC#3: custitem_lead_time_days is null. EC#7: Only one line. EC#1: All lines removed. TC-07 also covers removing line with longest lead time.

expectations.text: Testing section contains at least 3 test cases with specific input data and expected results
expectations.evidence: 15 test cases in §8, all with specific input and expected results (e.g. 'item has custitem_lead_time_days=7 → custbody_estimated_ship_date = today + 7 days').
